headlines.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
headlines.description: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

headlines.title: Moscow hit by largest Ukrainian attack since start of Russia's full-scale war
headlines.description: A refinery and a shopping centre were burning after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

headlines.title: Young women now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death after HPV jab
headlines.description: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

headlines.title: Church of England apologises for role in forced adoptions
headlines.description: Thousands of unmarried women were pressured to give up their babies between 1949 and 1976.

headlines.title: Lisa Nandy 'not satisfied' with Channel 4 response on Married at First Sight UK
headlines.description: BBC Panorama reported rape allegations from two cast members, allegations the men involved have denied.

headlines.title: Voters head to the polls for Makerfield by-election
headlines.description: There are 14 candidates vying to be the Greater Manchester constituency's new MP.

headlines.title: Harry Kane explains the strategy behind his stuttered penalty run-up
headlines.description: Harry Kane was able to give England the lead against Croatia from the penalty spot - but only thanks to the video assistant referee.

headlines.title: 'If we lose, we lose in our way' - how Tuchel inspired 'fearful' England
headlines.description: England players were on the receiving end of a half-time reproach from manager Thomas Tuchel as their World Cup campaign got off to a victorious start against Croatia.

headlines.title: Bostonians bemused as Scotland fans decorate city's statues with traffic cones
headlines.description: The practice is familiar to anyone from Glasgow, where the Duke of Wellington statue has been wearing a jaunty orange hat since the 1980s.

headlines.title: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after first game
headlines.description: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.

headlines.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
headlines.description: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

headlines.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
headlines.description: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

headlines.title: An ultra-rare Star Wars Lego collection went missing - it's sparked viral conspiracies
headlines.description: Bricks & Figs shops around the US have been receiving threats due to the missing Star Wars Lego set, the company says.

headlines.title: UK rapper thanks Linkin Park for 'changing my life' with freestyle shoutout
headlines.description: Mike Shinoda from the band says he spotted Megan Da Savage's rap on an independent music platform.

headlines.title: 'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
headlines.description: As the delivery vehicles increasing take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.

headlines.title: Simpler, older version of Stonehenge found three miles from famous site
headlines.description: The structure consisted of two posts that lined up with the solstices 5,000 years ago.

headlines.title: Amber heat health alerts issued with UK temperatures set to soar above 30C
headlines.description: Very warm weather is set to return to parts of the UK with temperatures of 30C plus expected, as Europe experiences a 'heat dome'.

headlines.title: Number of job vacancies hits five year-low
headlines.description: Latest figures suggest companies are becoming more cautious about taking on new staff.

headlines.title: The Ring and Lilo & Stitch actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
headlines.description: Chase died from sepsis following a bout with meningitis in a Los Angeles hospital, her longtime manager told the BBC.

headlines.title: Lil Nas X says he's 'feeling better' after rehab and bipolar diagnosis
headlines.description: The Old Town Road star was arrested last year for attacking police officers and later entered rehab.

headlines.title: Ancient 'Robin Hood' tree is dead, experts say
headlines.description: The Major Oak is thought to have stood for more than 1,000 years in the heart of Nottinghamshire's Sherwood Forest.

headlines.title: Why a Gen Z Londoner can graze sheep on land in the Welsh valleys
headlines.description: Caleb Tutt travelled from Lambeth to a hilltop in Llantrisant to claim his ancient birthright.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: How Will The Social Media Ban Change Children’s Lives?
headlines.description: Education secretary joins Newscast to discuss social media ban for under-16s.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Is Trump still fit for the job on his 80th birthday?
headlines.description: Donald Trump is now the second oldest president in US history

headlines.title: Layla Wright investigates Rachel Fulstow's life sentence
headlines.description: Layla Wright’s investigation into the conviction of a ‘killer couple'.

headlines.title: Tactical analysis: England look exciting but how can they tighten up?
headlines.description: England produced some exciting attacking football while beating Croatia 4-2 but why did they look so shaky defensively?

headlines.title: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after first game
headlines.description: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.

headlines.title: Lionesses will face Greece in World Cup play-offs
headlines.description: England will face Greece in round one of the 2027 Women's World Cup play-offs, with Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales also learning their opponents.

headlines.title: Calvin Harris & Commonwealths force Monday start to Celtic's title defence
headlines.description: Celtic open the 2026-27 Scottish Premiership season at home to Dundee on 3 August, with the champions saying they are disappointed to be starting on a Monday evening.

headlines.title: 'I never imagined something like this' - Nadal reflects on Wimbledon 2008 win
headlines.description: Rafael Nadal recalls his seismic five-set victory over Roger Federer in the 2008 Wimbledon final, arguably the greatest men's match ever played.
